title: Conflict Areas: Myanmar, Madagascar, Sudan
focus: Geographical facts and conflict context for high-profile crisis zones.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 027]
2. PLACES IN NEWS
2.1. CONFLICT AFFLICTED AREAS
Myanmar (Sagaing Region)
- Context: Over a million Rohingya Muslims from Myanmar now live as refugees in Bangladesh, where their human rights are being violated.
- Geographical Facts:
- Lies at the northwesternmost part of mainland Southeast Asia.
- Bordered by: China (North and Northeast), Laos (East), Thailand (South East), Bangladesh (West), India (Mizoram, Manipur, Nagaland and Arunachal Pradesh in the North West).
- Bordering water bodies: Andaman Sea (south) and by the Bay of Bengal (southwest).
- Rakhine State is the most western state in Myanmar where violence in 2020 displaced Rohingyas in large scale.
Madagascar (Antananarivo, the capital city)
- Context: Anti-government Gen Z protests in Madagascar have led to the fall of the government.
- Geographical Facts:
- Bordered by: Mauritius and Réunion (territory of France) (east). The French territory of Mayotte and the island country of Comoros (northwest).
- Located off the coast of East Africa in the Indian Ocean.
- The second-largest island country in the world.
- Approximately 90% of Madagascar’s flora and fauna is found nowhere else.
Sudan (Darfur, Khartoum, South Kordofan, Blue Nile States, El Fasher, capital of North Darfur)
- Context: Sudan plunged into a civil war in April 2023 after a vicious power struggle broke out between its Army and a powerful paramilitary group, the Rapid Support Force (RSF).
- Geographical Facts:
- Located in Northeast Africa.
- Third-largest country in Africa.
- Land Borders: Egypt (north), Eritrea and Ethiopia (East), South Sudan (South), Central African Republic and Chad (West), and Libya (Northwest).
- Maritime Boundary: Red Sea.
- The Nile River system (White Nile and Blue Nile) runs through Sudan.
- Refugee Camps/conflict areas in Sudan:
- Zamzam camp: Located south of El Fasher town, the state capital of North Darfur, Sudan.
